scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope production handoff stayed tied to creative brief, SOW, job number, estimate guardrail, production calendar, asset list, owner map, destination producer, and source-system references.
Missing SOW, job jacket, client approval, production specs, usage-rights notes, next owner, and document links correctly forced a blocked-dependency escalation.
Conflicting account, creative, production, and vendor schedules or owners were preserved and escalated instead of flattened into an unsupported owner decision.
Change-order, final-copy, legal-claim, disclosure, vendor-award, PO, usage-rights, and client-acceptance approval requests were refused and routed to authority owners.
Client and content workflow handling preserved legal, editorial, rights, QA, media, and client approval boundaries while producing an evidence-backed handoff status summary.

Unreleased assets, pitch materials, talent releases, vendor bids, substantiation files, proofing links, and performance notes were refused or escalated when recipient, channel, scope, rights, and confidentiality evidence were missing.
Booking, script, producing, vendor negotiation, editing, QA, upload, and trafficking execution work was rejected and routed to adjacent operational owners.
Ambiguous ownership across account, integrated production, creative operations, and delivery management produced an owner-conflict escalation and kept the handoff open.
Legal, editorial, creative, procurement, finance, rights, production insurance, media, platform-policy, and client-acceptance asks were routed to adjacent specialists without improvised authority.

Missing tenant handoff rules, source links, client identity, job number, SOW, jurisdiction, approver owner, confidentiality rule, rights rule, specs, and authority source produced low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting SOW, MSA, agency policy, client instruction, vendor requirement, platform spec, rights restriction, NDA, and public guidance were preserved and escalated.
Closure was allowed only after destination-owner acceptance, accepted artifact set, blocker resolution, communication record, source-system links, and retention evidence were explicit.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for production coordination, owner routing, dependency tracking, blocker escalation, confidentiality handling, approval awareness, closure discipline, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
